description: Airflow worker pod resource utilisation — answers: are my pods right-sized? which tasks consume the most? are any at OOM risk?

panels.title: 🚦 Health at a Glance
OOM Kills (time range)
panels.description: Total OOM kill events across all matched worker pods in the selected time range. Any value > 0 means pods are being killed due to memory exhaustion.

panels.title: Tasks >80% Memory Request
panels.description: Number of distinct dag_id/task_id combinations currently using more than 80% of their memory request. These tasks risk being throttled or evicted.

panels.title: Tasks >90% Memory Limit
panels.description: Number of distinct dag_id/task_id combinations using more than 90% of their memory limit. These are at immediate OOM risk.

panels.title: Tasks >80% CPU Request
panels.description: Number of distinct dag_id/task_id combinations consuming more than 80% of their CPU request. These tasks may be CPU-starved.

panels.title: Tasks >90% CPU Limit
panels.description: Number of distinct dag_id/task_id combinations consuming more than 90% of their CPU limit. These tasks are being throttled.

panels.title: 📦 Pod Size Distribution — Are Sizes Right-Sized?
Avg Memory Usage by Pod Size
panels.description: Average memory working set per pod size. Compare against the memory limit line to see how much headroom each size has.

panels.title: Memory % of Limit — Pod Size × Task (Heatmap)
panels.description: Each cell is the avg memory usage as % of limit for a dag/task at a given pod size. Dark red = near OOM. Use this to spot which tasks are mismatched to their pod size.

panels.title: 🧠 Memory Utilisation — by DAG / Task
Memory Usage % of Request — by DAG / Task
panels.description: How much of the memory request each dag/task is consuming over time. >80% (yellow) means the pod is using more than requested — it's relying on slack in the cluster. This is the right-sizing signal.

panels.title: Memory Usage % of Limit — by DAG / Task
panels.description: How close each dag/task is to its hard memory limit. >90% (red) means OOM kill is imminent. The limit is the hard ceiling set on the base container — crossing it triggers an OOM kill.

panels.title: Avg Memory Usage by Task — Trend Across Runs
panels.description: Average memory (bytes) consumed per dag/task over time. Each point represents the average across all concurrent runs of that task. Use this to spot tasks whose memory footprint is growing run-over-run.

panels.title: Avg Memory Usage by Task — Current Snapshot
panels.description: Point-in-time average memory per dag/task, ranked. Quickly shows which tasks consume the most memory right now, grouped by pod size for right-sizing context.

panels.title: ⚙️ CPU Utilisation — by DAG / Task
CPU Usage % of Request — by DAG / Task
panels.description: CPU consumption as a % of the requested CPU cores on the base container. >80% means the task is consuming more CPU than it asked for — it may be competing with other pods. The request is what Kubernetes uses to schedule the pod.

panels.title: CPU Usage % of Limit — by DAG / Task
panels.description: CPU consumption as a % of the CPU limit on the base container. >90% means the container is being CPU-throttled by the kernel cgroup — tasks will run slower but won't be killed. The limit is the hard ceiling Kubernetes enforces via cgroups.

panels.title: 💥 OOM Kill Detail
OOM Kills by DAG / Task / Pod Size
panels.description: OOM kill count per dag/task combination grouped by pod size over the selected time range. A task with OOM kills on 'small' but not 'medium' is a clear right-sizing signal — bump it up.

panels.title: OOM Kill Rate — Trend Over Time
panels.description: OOM kills over time per dag/task. A spike here means pods are being killed — correlate with the memory % of limit panel to confirm they were hitting the ceiling.

title: Airflow Worker Pods — Resource Utilisation
